decisions.briefing: Markets are in risk-off mode today with NASDAQ falling 1.3% and the S&P 500 down 0.4%. The screener returned no candidates, and our cash balance of $444.94 is below the $500 threshold for new buys. Holding all six positions and letting the code-enforced stop-losses and profit targets do their job.

market_education.summary_en: Markets sold off today as investors rotated away from risk assets amid renewed concerns over global trade policy uncertainty and mixed corporate earnings signals. Technology stocks bore the brunt of the decline, with NASDAQ falling 1.3%, as high-multiple growth names are most sensitive to any shift in risk appetite. The S&P 500 held up relatively better at -0.4%, reflecting defensive sector support from utilities and healthcare acting as a buffer against the tech-led pullback.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場避險情緒升溫，投資人因全球貿易政策不確定性再起及企業財報訊號參差而撤出風險資產。科技股首當其衝，那斯達克指數重挫1.3%，高本益比成長股對市場情緒轉變最為敏感。標普500相對抗跌，僅下跌0.4%，主因公用事業與醫療保健等防禦類股提供支撐，緩和了科技股帶動的跌勢。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' market is when investors become more cautious and move money away from volatile assets like growth stocks or crypto into safer havens like bonds, gold, or cash. You can spot it when high-growth indexes like NASDAQ fall more sharply than broader indexes like the S&P 500 — exactly what happened today. For a portfolio manager, risk-off days are a signal to pause new buys and protect existing gains rather than chase new positions.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資人因市場不確定性上升，將資金從高波動資產（如成長股、加密貨幣）撤出，轉入較安全的避風港，例如債券、黃金或現金。辨識方式很簡單：當那斯達克等成長型指數跌幅遠大於標普500時，就是避險情緒主導市場的信號——今日市場正是如此。對投資組合管理人而言，避險日的正確應對是暫停新買入，優先保護既有獲利，而非追逐新部位。
